dashboard_title: Henipavirus Vaccine and Therapeutics Development Dashboard

dashboard_summary.focus_statement: Active public-health countermeasure development is centered on Nipah virus and Hendra virus. Cedar virus is treated as a research surrogate/tool; Ghanaian bat/Kumasi, Mojiang, Angavokely, and Salt Gully rows are surveillance-only unless a source-backed product program appears.
dashboard_summary.human_license_statement: No human henipavirus vaccine or therapeutic is curated as licensed. Equivac® HeV is shown separately as a licensed veterinary vaccine for horses in Australia.
dashboard_summary.automation_statement: Automation refreshes trial-registry metadata, source checks, and publication-watch counts; curated maturity stage changes require human review.

curation_policy.highest_supported_stage: Rows are placed in the highest maturity stage supported by public evidence. Reserve/stockpile readiness is tracked as a separate attribute and does not outrank clinical stage.
curation_policy.human_vs_veterinary: Human, veterinary, therapeutic, and surveillance-only rows are separated with program_type and priority_group fields. Veterinary licensure is not human licensure.
curation_policy.gap_definition: Gap and surveillance lanes mean no source-backed active public product program was identified; they are not proof of unpublished absence.
curation_policy.auto_update_scope: Automation refreshes registry metadata, source availability, page titles, publication-search hits, and review flags; it does not silently promote curated stages without human review.
curation_policy.publication_status_policy: Trial completion, peer-reviewed results, and publication dates are tracked separately from trial phase so completed trials are not confused with published-result trials.

stage_status_legend.description: Registry or sponsor source indicates recruitment, enrolment, dosing, or launch is underway.
Trial completion or follow-up is reported, but peer-reviewed results are not yet curated.
Peer-reviewed or formal results publication is linked and publication date is captured.
Virus discovery, taxonomy, or surrogate research without active product development.
Approved animal product; not a human vaccine or therapeutic.

data_source_sections.label: Official trial registries
data_source_sections.description: Structured trial status endpoints are pulled from ClinicalTrials.gov v2 where NCT IDs exist; ISRCTN and EU CTIS pages are monitored through source checks until structured adapters are added.

stages.label: No active public vaccine or therapeutic program
stages.description: A clearly labelled gap lane: no source-backed active product program is represented for this target, indication, or species. This is not proof that no unpublished work exists.

stages.label: Surveillance / research tool only
stages.description: Virus discovery, taxonomy, receptor, tropism, diagnostic, or surrogate-model work without an active public vaccine/therapeutic development path.

stages.label: Preclinical / animal-model evidence
stages.description: Platform or product evidence in cells, mice, hamsters, ferrets, pigs, cats, or non-human primates without a verified human clinical trial.

stages.label: IND-enabling / clinical-entry preparation
stages.description: Good-laboratory-practice, manufacturing, toxicology, dose-selection, or funded work intended to enable first-in-human testing, but no confirmed public clinical start.

stages.label: Phase I planned / registered
stages.description: First-in-human trial is announced, funded, registered, or scheduled, but public evidence does not yet support active enrolment or completion.

stages.label: Phase I started / ongoing
stages.description: First-in-human testing has publicly started or is actively recruiting/enrolling healthy volunteers.

stages.label: Phase I completed / results pending
stages.description: Phase I follow-up or registry completion is reported, but peer-reviewed clinical results are not yet published.

stages.label: Phase I results published
stages.description: First-in-human safety, tolerability, and immunogenicity/safety results have been published or otherwise formally reported.

stages.label: Phase II planned / funded
stages.description: Affected-area or mid-stage testing is publicly funded, announced, or in setup, but public sources do not yet confirm launch/enrolment.

stages.label: Phase II ongoing
stages.description: Mid-stage or affected-area Phase II testing has launched, with geography, enrolment, and population details publicly described.

stages.label: Phase III / pivotal
stages.description: Pivotal efficacy testing. No human henipavirus vaccine candidate is currently curated in this stage.

stages.label: Licensed for human use
stages.description: Licensed human vaccine or therapeutic for the stated henipavirus indication. No row is currently curated in this lane.

stages.label: Licensed veterinary countermeasure
stages.description: Licensed animal-health product; shown separately from human licensure to avoid overstating human countermeasure availability.

records.lineage_or_scope: Bangladesh exposure setting / NiV-B-focused candidate
records.platform: Non-replicating chimpanzee adenovirus vector (ChAdOx1) expressing Nipah virus Bangladesh antigen
records.sponsor_or_steward: University of Oxford Pandemic Sciences Institute, CEPI, icddr,b, Serum Institute of India
records.setting: Phase I in Oxford; Phase IIa safety/immunogenicity study in Bangladesh; investigational reserve manufacturing by SII
records.trial_status: Phase II ongoing; Phase I one-year follow-up complete, results expected
records.clinical_phase_detail: World-first Phase II Nipah vaccine trial launched in Bangladesh in December 2025; planned enrolment 306 healthy adults aged 18–55. Earlier Oxford Phase I enrolled 51 adults aged 18–55.
records.publication_status: Phase I results expected; Phase II not yet read out

trial_locations.site: Oxford Vaccine Group / University of Oxford

trial_locations.role: Phase IIa affected-region safety and immunogenicity trial

funding.grant_or_award: SII process development, manufacturing, Phase II supply, investigational reserve
funding.note: Funding of up to US$7.3M supports manufacturing and up to 100,000 investigational reserve doses.
CEPI describes a broader US$150M Nipah R&D portfolio.

records.reserve_or_stockpile_status: Investigational reserve of up to 100,000 doses planned for emergency-use deployment; not human licensure.
records.status_summary: Updated to Phase II ongoing. ChAdOx1 NipahB is the most advanced public Nipah vaccine candidate, with affected-region testing now launched in Bangladesh and a CEPI/SII investigational reserve pathway.
records.next_milestone_or_gap: Phase II safety/immunogenicity readout, Phase I publication, correlates/assay work, emergency-use governance, and larger at-risk population strategy.
records.evidence_class: Phase II ongoing + investigational reserve manufacturing
